# Dutch Party Landscape — Comparative Positions on Four Priority Areas

## Summary

This file maps D66, GroenLinks–PvdA, Partij voor de Dieren (PvdD), NSC, CDA, and VVD across the user's four priority areas, using their 2025 election programmes as primary sources. These are the parties explicitly named in the brief for comparison against Volt. All positions reflect the 2025 election cycle (elections held October 29, 2025); D66 subsequently entered government (Jetten cabinet, sworn in February 2026) alongside VVD and CDA, which creates a distinction between programme promises and current governing commitments.

**The Jetten cabinet** (D66/VVD/CDA minority, supported by SGP, ChristenUnie, and the Markuszower Group of ex-PVV MPs) governs from a five-point agenda: housing, nitrogen, economic policy, international security, migration/asylum. Volt is in opposition with GroenLinks-PvdA, PvdD, NSC, and others.

### Summary matrix (high-level)

| Area | Volt | D66 | GL-PvdA | PvdD | NSC | CDA | VVD |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Climate ambition | 2040 neutrality (binding) | 2050 trajectory; 2030 targets | Penalise polluters; megastall ban | 2030 neutral; 5% GDP | 4 nuclear plants; gradual N reduction | Mixed portfolio; 2 nuclear | Growth-first; scrap CO2 charges |
| EU federalism | Federal EU + constitution | Deep federalism; elected Commission president | Pro-EU; national sovereignty on defense | Not a priority | "Limit and strengthen"; subsidiary | Cooperative; strategic autonomy | Coordination, not federalism |
| European army | Yes — under EP oversight | Yes — long term | National sovereignty emphasis | Not prominent | No specific position found | Not prominent | No — prefers joint procurement |
| Housing: mortgage deduction | Abolish quickly | Phase out with income tax cut | Gradual phase-out | Maintain below NHG; phase above | Preserve | Long-term phase-out with income tax cut | Maintain entirely |
| Housing: construction | European-level solution | 10 new cities | 100k/year via land repurposing | Nature-inclusive; 4m² green per 1m² built | €2bn acceleration fund | 100k/year; neighbourhood coordination | 30 large districts; market-led |
| Wealth/profit tax | Wealth tax >€57k; more profit tax | Not specified in search results | More wealth + profit tax | Speculative sales ban; max property holdings | Preserve existing; lower transfer tax | Not specified | Oppose wealth tax |
| Democracy reform | Constitutional court; 250-seat TK; citizen assembly | Democratise EU; directly elect Commission president | Not prominent in found sources | Human rights treaties | Good governance focus | Bridge-builder | Individual freedom focus |
| Digital rights | Algorithm register; ban facial recognition; no ethnic profiling | Big Tech accountability; data sovereignty | Not prominent in found sources | LGBTQI+ and protest rights protection | Cybersecurity focus | Not prominent | Not prominent |

## Evidence

### D66

- [D66 Verkiezingsprogramma 2025–2030 — "Het kan wél"](https://d66.nl/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/D66-Verkiezingsprogramma-2025-2030-2.pdf) (accessed 2026-04-19) — Full programme PDF (not fetched in full; accessed via summary and sub-pages).
- [D66 klimaat samenvatting — NVDE](https://www.nvde.nl/samenvatting-d66-verkiezingsprogramma-klimaat-energie/) (accessed 2026-04-19) — Climate: 5–6% annual emissions reduction; 2050 prosperity without pollution; offshore wind as base; openness to nuclear; 1 million rental retrofits by 2030; fossil subsidy reduction; sector-specific phase-out dates.
- [D66 Europa — defensie en veiligheid](https://d66.nl/verkiezingsprogramma/europa-defensie-internationale-samenwerking-en-migratie/europa-dat-beschermt-en-vrijheid/) (accessed 2026-04-19) — EU: deep federalism; directly elected Commission President; EP can dismiss individual Commissioners; European army in long term; EU Defense Union for 23 NATO-member states.
- [D66 samenvatting — d66.nl](https://d66.nl/verkiezingsprogramma/samenvatting/) (accessed 2026-04-19) — Housing: fair housing market; phase out mortgage deduction while lowering income tax; substantial social housing investment. Digital: Big Tech values above profits; employee and user control over products and data. Democracy: everyone can vote, speak, make decisions.
- [Viisi — woningmarkt 2025](https://www.viisi.nl/nieuws/verkiezingsprogrammas-2025-woningmarkt-en-hypotheekrenteaftrek-per-partij/) (accessed 2026-04-19) — D66 housing: 10 new cities; phase out mortgage deduction while lowering income tax; landlord licensing nationwide; debt-neutral study loans for mortgage calculation; rent increase limits.
- [A closer look at D66 — Brussels Report](https://www.brusselsreport.eu/2025/11/13/a-closer-look-at-d66-the-winner-of-the-dutch-elections/) (accessed 2026-04-19) — Confirms D66 is "second most pro-EU party after Volt." Joint debt borrowing position notable given Netherlands' historical resistance. Article notes D66's EU federalism emerged partly from failed domestic reform objectives.

### GroenLinks–PvdA

- [GroenLinks–PvdA — Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/GroenLinks%E2%80%93PvdA) (accessed 2026-04-19) — Alliance formed 2023; internal referenda June 2025 approved full merger into "Progressief Nederland" scheduled June 13, 2026. Ecological sustainability + social justice. 2025 election: 20 seats (lost 5 from 2023).
- [BNNVARA klimaatstandpunten 2025](https://www.bnnvara.nl/artikelen/verkiezingen-en-klimaat-standpunten-partijen) (accessed 2026-04-19) — Climate: end fossil subsidies; "polluters pay for their emissions"; affordable climate ticket (€59/month unlimited PT); ban PFAS and agricultural pesticides; force buyouts of major emitters; eliminate megastalls.
- [Viisi — woningmarkt 2025](https://www.viisi.nl/nieuws/verkiezingsprogrammas-2025-woningmarkt-en-hypotheekrenteaftrek-per-partij/) (accessed 2026-04-19) — Housing: 100k annual units via land repurposing; exempt housing corporations from taxes; gradual mortgage deduction phase-out; revenue funds construction; "Startersfonds" covers 30% of purchase price (repaid at resale); legal rent ceiling.
- [Brusselse Nieuwe — defensie vergelijking](https://brusselsenieuwe.nl/wat-zeggen-politieke-partijen-in-hun-verkiezingsprogramma-over-het-europese-defensiebeleid/) (accessed 2026-04-19) — GL-PvdA emphasises national sovereignty in defense decision-making; supports NATO; 3.5% GDP spending.
- [D66 vergelijkt met GL-PvdA op woningmarkt](https://groenlinkspvda.nl/partijvergelijker/partij-voor-de-dieren/) (accessed 2026-04-19) — General social/ecological alignment with PvdD; specific cross-party comparisons limited to municipal context in available source.
- Note: GL-PvdA formally merging into Progressief Nederland (June 2026). Future positioning under new name is not yet documented.

### Partij voor de Dieren (PvdD)

- [PvdD Verkiezingsprogramma 2025 — partijvoordedieren.nl](https://www.partijvoordedieren.nl/partijprogrammas/tweede-kamerverkiezingen-2025) (accessed 2026-04-19) — Landing page; 160-page programme.
- [BNNVARA klimaatstandpunten 2025](https://www.bnnvara.nl/artikelen/verkiezingen-en-klimaat-standpunten-partijen) (accessed 2026-04-19) — Climate: most aggressive target — climate-neutral by 2030; 5% GDP for climate/nature crisis; halt fossil subsidies immediately; phase out petrochemistry, aviation, industrial livestock; close coal plants and Tata Steel; ban PFAS; reject nuclear expansion.
- [Viisi — woningmarkt 2025](https://www.viisi.nl/nieuws/verkiezingsprogrammas-2025-woningmarkt-en-hypotheekrenteaftrek-per-partij/) (accessed 2026-04-19) — Housing: nature-inclusive building (4m² greenery per m² built); maintain mortgage deduction below NHG, phase out above; 5-year rent freeze; cap private sector increases; ban evictions for debt; maximum property holdings per individual; prohibit speculative short-term sales.
- [RefugeeHelp — Dutch political parties overview](https://www.refugeehelp.nl/en/asylum-seeker/article/100547-what-political-parties-are-there-in-the-netherlands) (accessed 2026-04-19) — PvdD: eco-centrism at core; animal rights; radical planetary protection; campaigns against industrial agriculture.
- Digital rights: PvdD supports LGBTQI+ protection and right to protest (from ikformeer.nl data); no specific digital-rights platform found comparable to Volt's.
- EU integration: PvdD does not emphasise EU federalism; its Euro-programme is not a defining party identity issue in available sources.

### NSC (Nieuw Sociaal Contract)

- [NSC — Wikipedia](https://nl.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nieuw_Sociaal_Contract) (accessed 2026-04-19) — Founded by Pieter Omtzigt 2023; centre-right; left coalition after Schoof cabinet collapse August 2025 over sanctions against Israel.
- [New Social Contract — Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_Social_Contract) (accessed 2026-04-19) — "Limit and strengthen Europe": less detail-level regulation, more cooperation on defense and internal market.
- [BNNVARA klimaatstandpunten 2025](https://www.bnnvara.nl/artikelen/verkiezingen-en-klimaat-standpunten-partijen) (accessed 2026-04-19) — Climate: 4 new nuclear plants; no new megastall permits; targeted buyout programs; significant nitrogen reduction by 2035; partial fossil fuel tax phase-out.
- [Viisi — woningmarkt 2025](https://www.viisi.nl/nieuws/verkiezingsprogrammas-2025-woningmarkt-en-hypotheekrenteaftrek-per-partij/) (accessed 2026-04-19) — Housing: €2bn acceleration fund; modular housing; preserve mortgage deduction; cap social rent to inflation; private: inflation +1%; lower transfer tax; starter loan programs.
- NSC EU/defense: subsidiarity principle; conscription reintroduction (Scandinavian model); limited federalism appetite. Did not find specific digital rights or democracy programme beyond "good governance" framing.

### CDA

- [CDA verkiezingsprogramma — cda.nl](https://www.cda.nl/verkiezingsprogramma/) (accessed 2026-04-19) — "Oplossingen voor Nederland" programme.
- [CDA klimaat samenvatting — NVDE](https://www.nvde.nl/samenvatting-cda-verkiezingsprogramma-klimaat-energie/) (accessed 2026-04-19) — Climate: balanced energy mix (nuclear, wind, solar, hydrogen, biomass, geothermal); 2 nuclear plants; sector-specific industrial agreements; cut regulations 20%; nitrogen reduction by 2035 through voluntary farmer agreements.
- [BNNVARA klimaatstandpunten 2025](https://www.bnnvara.nl/artikelen/verkiezingen-en-klimaat-standpunten-partijen) (accessed 2026-04-19) — CO2 airport cap; two new nuclear plants; reformed nitrogen fund.
- [Viisi — woningmarkt 2025](https://www.viisi.nl/nieuws/verkiezingsprogrammas-2025-woningmarkt-en-hypotheekrenteaftrek-per-partij/) (accessed 2026-04-19) — Housing: 100k/year neighbourhood-by-neighbourhood; long-term mortgage deduction phase-out with income tax cut; keep private rental attractive; reserved units for key workers.
- [Brusselse Nieuwe — defensie vergelijking](https://brusselsenieuwe.nl/wat-zeggen-politieke-partijen-in-hun-verkiezingsprogramma-over-het-europese-defensiebeleid/) (accessed 2026-04-19) — Defense: 3.5% + 1.5% GDP; no specific position on European army found; strategic autonomy framing.
- CDA is now in government (Jetten cabinet). Their governing positions may diverge from the 2025 programme on some specifics.

### VVD

- [VVD — Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/People%27s_Party_for_Freedom_and_Democracy) (accessed 2026-04-19) — Centre-right, economic liberal, individual freedom.
- [BNNVARA klimaatstandpunten 2025](https://www.bnnvara.nl/artikelen/verkiezingen-en-klimaat-standpunten-partijen) (accessed 2026-04-19) — Climate: "Klimaat- en Groeiwet" replacing climate law with growth considerations; scrap CO2 pricing; accelerate nuclear; binding nitrogen targets per hectare; preserve industrial competitiveness.
- [Viisi — woningmarkt 2025](https://www.viisi.nl/nieuws/verkiezingsprogrammas-2025-woningmarkt-en-hypotheekrenteaftrek-per-partij/) (accessed 2026-04-19) — Housing: 30 large-scale districts under state direction; streamline permits; maintain mortgage deduction entirely; eliminate rental restrictions; expand starter mortgage programs.
- [Brusselse Nieuwe — defensie vergelijking](https://brusselsenieuwe.nl/wat-zeggen-politieke-partijen-in-hun-verkiezingsprogramma-over-het-europese-defensiebeleid/) (accessed 2026-04-19) — Defense: 3.5% GDP; opposed to EU army; prefers joint procurement and coordination within NATO framework.
- VVD also now in government (Jetten cabinet). Programme positions on housing (maintaining deduction) may be in tension with governing partners.

## Uncertainty / conflicting sources

- **D66/VVD/CDA governing positions vs. programme**: The Jetten cabinet coalition agreement (*Aan de slag*, January 2026) focuses on housing, nitrogen, economic policy, international security, and migration. Specific programme deviations are not fully documented in available sources. The coalition agreement text was not retrieved. D66, VVD, and CDA positions above reflect their election programmes; actual governing choices may differ.
- **GL-PvdA merger**: The party is in the process of becoming Progressief Nederland (formal merger June 2026). No policy programme for the new entity is yet available. All GL-PvdA positions above are from the 2025 election cycle and may not reflect the merged party's definitive platform.
- **NSC digital rights and democracy**: NSC's specific positions on algorithm regulation, facial recognition, and digital rights were not recoverable from available sources. NSC frames itself as a "good governance" party but the content of that framing on digital matters is not documented here.
- **Comparability of sources**: Not all parties have equally detailed third-party summaries. Climate and housing positions are better documented than digital rights and democracy positions for the comparison parties, because NVDE and Viisi produced systematic cross-party analyses on exactly those two topics.
